evals.prompt: /draft jesse AI automation for small marketing teams
evals.expected_output: Full MODE 1 pipeline: step labels shown, angle options generated, human review gate displayed, final READY TO POST output block with Jesse's voice applied (specific tools named, direct tone, no banned vocabulary)

assertions.text: Output contains all expected MODE 1 step labels: EXECUTIVE LOADED, ANGLE OPTIONS, DRAFT COMPLETE, READY FOR YOUR REVIEW, and READY TO POST
Step 2 shows at least 2 distinct angle options, each with a 'Why it works' or equivalent rationale
A human review gate is displayed — READY FOR YOUR REVIEW section or equivalent hard stop prompt before final output
Output contains the final READY TO POST block with word count, emoji count, and scheduling recommendation

Final post does not contain any of these words: game-changing, leveraging, crucial, impactful, utilize, utilization, moreover, furthermore, additionally, revolutionary, transformative, rapidly evolving, unprecedented
Final post mentions at least one specific tool by name relevant to Jesse's stack (Clay, HubSpot, Zapier, Claude, Framer, Jitter, or similar)
The final post body (not the metadata block) contains no bulleted or dashed list items — prose only
The final post contains 2 or fewer emojis total

evals.prompt:
/react ryan

Source text: "Engineering teams at mid-size companies are spending an average of $800–$1,200 per developer per year on AI coding tools — Copilot, Cursor, Claude, and others. But in a new survey of 400 CTOs, 67% said they couldn't directly tie that spend to measurable productivity gains. Most cited 'developer feel' and 'perceived velocity' as the primary evidence. One CTO said, 'We're basically paying a premium for vibes and hoping it shows up in the output someday.'"
evals.expected_output: MODE 3 pipeline: source ingested, Ryan's POV drafted (not a summary of source), QA pipeline applied, final output block with Ryan's voice (relaxed, direct, fun, specific engineering experience)

assertions.text: Output contains MODE 3 step labels: EXECUTIVE LOADED, SOURCE INGESTED, and READY TO POST (or equivalent reaction draft + final output labels)
A SOURCE INGESTED step is present that extracts a 1–3 sentence core premise from the input text
Final post does not contain phrases that indicate source-summarizing: 'according to', 'the survey found', 'a recent study', 'the article says', 'research shows'
Final post does not contain Ryan's banned vocabulary: driving technical strategy, architecting the future, transformational infrastructure, game-changing, impactful
Output contains the final READY TO POST block with Ryan's name and scheduling recommendation
Final post contains at least one specific practitioner detail: a real tool name, a specific number/cost figure, or a first-person experience reference

evals.prompt: /qa jesse [AI-sounding draft with: rapidly evolving, leveraging, crucial, impactful, revolutionizing, utilization, unprecedented, fostering, 3 emojis]
evals.expected_output: MODE 4 QA pipeline: all 3 passes run, banned AI vocabulary removed, emojis trimmed to 2 or fewer, voice rewritten to match Jesse's direct/practitioner tone

assertions.text: Output contains MODE 4 step labels: EXECUTIVE LOADED with Voice DNA loaded, and a QA pipeline summary
Output shows a QA summary referencing at least 2 of the 3 passes (PASS 1 fact check, PASS 2 voice, PASS 3 humanization)
Final post does NOT contain any of these words from the original draft: leveraging, crucial, impactful, revolutionizing, utilization, unprecedented, fostering, 'rapidly evolving', 'align with'
Final post contains 2 or fewer emojis (original draft had 3: rocket, sparkles, stars)
Final post body contains no bulleted or dashed list items — Jesse's Voice DNA requires prose only
Output contains the final READY TO POST block with Jesse's name and word count
Final post reads as direct and practitioner-specific — mentions real tools by name OR contains a specific personal observation, not generic marketing language
